title: Control of Breathing
authors: Estelle B. Gauda & Richard J. Martin

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Control of Breathing?
Breathing ใน preterm infant = fetal breathing ที่ยังไม่ stable: episodic, hypoxia-prone, bradycardia-prone. เข้าใจ mechanism = รักษา AOP ถูกจุด. (1) Pre-Bötzinger complex (PBC) คือ pacemaker ของ respiratory rhythm — ถ้า NK1R ลดใน preterm → SIDS risk เพิ่ม; (2) GABA excitatory → inhibitory switch ยังไม่สมบูรณ์ใน preterm — GABA ยัง excitatory → unstable breathing; (3) Caffeine ออกฤทธิ์ผ่าน A1/A2a adenosine receptor blockade; CAP trial (Schmidt NEJM 2007) ลด BPD + improve motor outcomes; (4) CCHS = PHOX2B mutation — phenotype ขึ้นกับจำนวน polyalanine repeats; (5) Intermittent hypoxia จาก AOP → ROP, BPD, neurodevelopmental impairment.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย anatomy ของ brainstem respiratory network: PBC, Bötzinger complex, VRC, NTS, pontine group
อธิบาย GABA developmental switch จาก excitatory → inhibitory และผลต่อ respiratory control ใน preterm
อธิบาย neurochemical modulators ของ respiratory rhythm: adenosine, serotonin, opioid, substance P
เข้าใจ maturation ของ CO2 sensitivity (central) และ O2 sensitivity (peripheral — carotid body)
อธิบาย hypoxic ventilatory depression (hypoxic roll-off) และ role ของ adenosine
แยก apnea of prematurity: central / obstructive / mixed; pathophysiology และ clinical consequences
รู้ therapeutic approaches: CPAP, caffeine (loading 20 mg/kg, maintenance 5–10 mg/kg/day), NIPPV
อธิบาย CCHS: PHOX2B gene, PARM vs NPARM, clinical severity, Haddad syndrome
อธิบาย laryngeal chemoreflex (LCR) และความสัมพันธ์กับ SIDS, nicotine, hyperthermia
รู้ consequences ของ intermittent hypoxia: ROP, BPD, cardiovascular, neurodevelopmental
1. Brainstem Respiratory Network — Architecture
1.1 Anatomic Regions

Respiratory neurons แบ่งเป็น 3 areas: (1) Dorsal respiratory group (DRG) ใน nucleus tractus solitarii (NTS) — sensory integration; (2) Ventral respiratory column (VRC) — rhythmogenesis + pattern formation; (3) Pontine respiratory group (dorsolateral pons) — fine-tuning

VRC contains: pre-Bötzinger complex (PBC) + Bötzinger complex + rostral/caudal VRG. PBC = respiratory pacemaker — NK1R + somatostatin neurons; synaptically coupled excitatory cells with intrinsic bursting properties; progressive PBC destruction → death
Post-inspiratory complex (PiCo): recently discovered autonomous rhythm-generator; medial to nucleus ambiguus; controls post-inspiratory activity
Retrotrapezoid nucleus (RTN): ventral medullary surface; CO2/H+ chemosensor; glutamatergic → drives VRC neurons; contains PHOX2B-expressing neurons
1.2 Respiratory Muscles
Pump muscles: diaphragm (C3-C5 phrenic nerve), intercostal, abdominal. Upper airway: alae nares, pharyngeal, laryngeal (nucleus ambiguus/hypoglossal)
Post-inspiratory activity → diaphragm + thyroarytenoid contract → grunting = audible post-inspiratory upper airway muscle activity; sign of low lung volume (RDS, atelectasis)
Chest wall highly compliant ใน preterm → paradoxical breathing in active sleep → mixed apnea from obstructed inspiratory efforts
2. Neurochemical Control — Neurotransmitters & Neuromodulators
2.1 Excitatory vs Inhibitory
Glutamate: major excitatory NT ตลอดชีวิต (NMDA, AMPA receptors)
GABA + Glycine: major inhibitory NT ใน adult — แต่ EXCITATORY ใน early development. เพราะ NKCC1 > KCC2 ratio ใน immature neuron → intracellular Cl- สูง → GABA binding → membrane depolarization

With maturation: KCC2 expression ↑ → NKCC1/KCC2 ratio reverses → intracellular Cl- ลด → GABA binding → hyperpolarization (inhibitory). Switch ยังไม่สมบูรณ์ใน preterm → less inhibitory braking → unstable breathing
Midazolam (GABAA agonist) → myoclonic jerks ใน preterm เพราะ GABA ยัง excitatory บางส่วน (Ozcan Pediatr Neurol 2015)
2.2 Key Neuromodulators

rows: ↓ NK1R binding ใน SIDS preterm brains

blocks.text: 3. Genetic Mutations — Respiratory Control Disorders
3.1 CCHS — Congenital Central Hypoventilation Syndrome

CCHS (Ondine's Curse) — PHOX2B Gene Mutations
Incidence: 1:200,000 live births. Autosomal dominant. > 90% มี PHOX2B mutations (chromosome 4). PHOX2B จำเป็นต่อการพัฒนา carotid body, NTS, catecholaminergic neurons, RTN chemoreceptors. Classic presentation: adequate ventilation awake แต่ profound hypoventilation ระหว่าง sleep + impaired CO2 response. PARM (polyalanine repeat mutations): 20/25 mildest → 20/28-32 ต้อง continuous ventilation. NPARM (frameshift): Haddad syndrome = CCHS + Hirschsprung disease + neuroblastoma. Respiratory stimulants ไม่ได้ผล; diaphragmatic pacing option.
PHOX2B expression ใน RTN → RTN เป็น central chemoreceptor ที่ release glutamate ↑ breathing during hypercapnia
Hirschsprung: 80% ของ NPARM patients; 10% ของ PARM. Neural crest tumors: 1% PARM vs 41% NPARM
3.2 Rett Syndrome & Prader-Willi
Rett syndrome: MECP2 gene (X-linked) — normal at birth → progressive deterioration. ↓ NE + serotonin in medulla → prolonged apnea + hyperventilation. Buspirone/fluoxetine case reports
Prader-Willi syndrome: NDN gene (chromosome 15) → brainstem serotonergic abnormalities → breathing abnormalities at birth + beyond; sleep-disordered breathing common
3.3 SIDS — Brainstem Serotonergic Deficiency
SIDS/SUID: ↑ long allele SLC6A4 variant (serotonin transporter) → ↑ reuptake → relative serotonin deficiency → dysregulation cardiorespiratory system
Non-Hispanic Blacks: 2.0× higher SIDS/SUID incidence vs Whites; long allele polymorphism more frequent in African-Americans
Preterm infants who died of SIDS: ↓ NK1R binding in NTS + PGCL (homologue of PBC) — esp. male sex (Moon AAP 2016)
4. Chemical Control of Breathing — CO2 and O2
4.1 Central CO2/H+ Chemoreceptors
Central chemoreceptors ใน RTN + medullary raphe + locus ceruleus depolarize ↑ CO2/↓pH → drive VRC neurons → ↑ ventilation
Maturation: CO2 sensitivity present at birth แต่ reduced vs adult; preterm มี lower CO2 sensitivity → ↓ central drive → more AOP. Reaches mature level ~4 weeks postnatal age (Rigatto Pediatrics 1975)
Predominant effect: ↑ tidal volume (not RR) in response to CO2 — fetal pattern, maintained early postnatally
4.2 Peripheral Arterial Chemoreceptors — Carotid Body
Carotid body ที่ bifurcation of carotid artery: Type 1 (glomus cells — chemosensitive, contain NTs), Type 2 (glial-like), microganglion, vessels
Major excitatory NT ใน carotid body: ATP (rapid cation ligand-gated ion channel on carotid sinus nerve). Dopamine = inhibitory neuromodulator
Hypoxic sensitivity: NOT functional first few days post-birth in most mammals (carotid body resetting after O2 tension ↑ at birth); mature ~2–3 wk postnatally
Hyperoxia (Dejours test): ↓ ventilation; greatest drop in preterm > term > adult → peripheral chemoreceptors more active in preterm baseline
4.3 Hypoxic Ventilatory Depression (Hypoxic Roll-Off)

Hypoxic Roll-Off — ทำไมทารก Preterm จึง Apnea ระหว่าง Hypoxia?
Ventilation เพิ่มใน 30 sec แรก (peripheral chemoreceptor response) แล้ว ลดต่ำกว่า baseline ภายใน 2–3 นาที = hypoxic ventilatory depression. กลไก: adenosine ↑ ใน brain during hypoxia (2.3× ใน fetal sheep; 100× ในหนู ischemia); A1 receptor activation → ↓ phrenic output; A2a receptor → excite GABAergic interneurons → ↑ inhibition. Caffeine/aminophylline block A1+A2a → attenuate hypoxic depression. Most pronounced ใน newborn; more severe ใน preterm; associated with apnea.
5. Sleep State Effects on Breathing

Active sleep (AS) = REM: ↑ irregular breathing, paradoxical chest wall motion, upper airway dilator muscle inhibition → obstructive apnea prone. Preterm spend 80% time in AS at 30–32 wk → 50% at term → 20% in adult
Quiet sleep (QS) = NREM: smooth, regular breathing driven by chemical control; CO2 sensitivity higher. CCHS worst in QS
Arousal latency to hypoxia: longer in AS vs QS; longer in preterm vs term; recent data: long apnea or hypoxia often fails to elicit arousal in preterm (Seppä-Moilanen Pediatr Res 2021)
Prone position → ↑ QS proportion → longer arousal time → ↑ SIDS risk (do NOT place prone for sleep)
6. Laryngeal Chemoreflex (LCR)
Most potent airway-protective reflex — triggered by liquid at larynx (hypo-osmolar, low Cl- content)

Mature response (adult): cough + arousal + swallowing. Immature response (newborn): apnea + laryngospasm + bradycardia + ↓ O2 — life-threatening. Afferent: superior laryngeal nerve → NTS → (1) nucleus ambiguus → thyroarytenoid → laryngospasm; (2) ↓ phrenic → apnea; (3) cardiac vagal neurons → bradycardia
Hypoxia enhances LCR severity (Wennergren Acta Paediatr 1989). Hyperthermia prolongs LCR (Xia J Appl Physiol 2008). Prenatal nicotine exaggerates LCR (Xia Respir Physiol Neurobiol 2010)
GER → LCR apnea: timing studies show apnea + GER rarely temporally linked; avoid H2 blockers in preterm (↑ NEC, mortality) — Terrin Pediatrics 2012
7. Apnea of Prematurity (AOP)
7.1 Definition and Classification
No consensus definition: proposed = respiratory pause > 15 sec หรือ shorter pause + bradycardia + desaturation
Periodic breathing: cycles 10–15 sec with pauses 5–10 sec = normal, no intervention needed

rows: คิดเป็น % ของ Long Apnea
Chest wall motion ± airflow absent; glottis/pharynx obstructed

blocks.text: 7.2 Pathophysiology
Greater inhibitory vs excitatory influence on PBC → episodic breathing; ↑ adenosine; GABA still excitatory in some circuits
Compliant chest wall → ↓ FRC → rapid O2 desaturation after short pause; ↑ upper airway compliance → obstruction during AS
Triggers: sepsis (IL-1 → prostaglandin E2 → brainstem respiratory depression); anemia (↓ O2 delivery); hyperbilirubinemia; thermal instability; inflammation
Resolution: usually 36–40 weeks PCA; most normalize by 43–44 weeks PCA. Premature discharge home monitoring option ถ้า occasional bradycardia only
7.3 Consequences of Intermittent Hypoxia

Intermittent Hypoxia — Long-Term Consequences
Poets JAMA 2015 (N=2500 preterm): intermittent hypoxemia/bradycardia ≥ 1 event at 36 wk → ↑ late death/disability. Di Fiore J Pediatr 2010: ↑ hypoxemic episodes → ↑ severe ROP. Raffay Pediatr Res 2019: ↑ IH events → BPD. Possible mechanism: neuronal plasticity alteration → persistent ↑ sympathetic tone + airway reactivity → cardiovascular, respiratory, neurodevelopmental morbidity long-term.
8. Treatment — AOP
8.1 CPAP
CPAP 4–6 cmH2O: splints upper airway (pharynx) + ↑ FRC → prolong time to desaturation; effective for mixed/obstructive > pure central apnea
NIPPV (nasal intermittent positive pressure): reduces reintubation for persistent apnea post-extubation (Lemyre Cochrane 2014 CD003212)
8.2 Caffeine — Mainstay Therapy

CAP Trial — Caffeine for Apnea of Prematurity (Schmidt NEJM 2007)
Schmidt B et al. N Engl J Med. 2007;357:1893. N = 2006 preterm. Caffeine vs placebo. Short-term: ↓ PMA at O2 discontinuation, ↓ MV duration, ↓ BPD (OR 0.63; NNT 10). 18-month follow-up: ↓ cerebral palsy + cognitive delay (not sustained at 5 yr but ↓ developmental coordination disorder). Early caffeine (< 3 days) more effective than late initiation. Dose: loading 20 mg/kg caffeine citrate IV, maintenance 5–10 mg/kg/day q24h. Mechanism: competitive A1/A2a adenosine receptor blockade → ↑ central drive, ↑ CO2 sensitivity, ↓ hypoxic depression, ↓ diaphragmatic fatigue. 11-yr follow-up (Schmidt JAMA Pediatr 2017): sustained ↓ motor impairment. Serum levels subtherapeutic ~11–12 days after stopping.
Adverse effects (usually at toxic levels): tachycardia, dysrhythmia, feeding intolerance, seizures. Mild diuresis. ↑ metabolic rate ~20% → ↑ caloric needs
High-dose caffeine: pilot RCT (McPherson Pediatr Res 2015) → associated with cerebellar injury on MRI — caution with doses > standard
Doxapram: under study in Europe; not recommended without safety data
9. NICU Practical — Apnea ที่ NICU
AOP เพิ่มขึ้นใน first few days: ตรวจหา sepsis (CBC, CRP, blood culture), anemia (Hct), electrolyte, glucose, temperature instability, hyperbilirubinemia
เริ่ม caffeine: ทุก preterm < 34 wk ที่มี clinical apnea ± prophylactic ใน < 28 wk. Early initiation (< 3 days) ดีที่สุด (Lodha JAMA Pediatr 2015)
Caffeine cessation timing: ส่วนใหญ่หยุดที่ 33–34 wk PCA ถ้า apnea resolve; extended use ถ้ายัง symptomatic (Rhein JAMA Pediatr 2014: ↓ IH events with extended therapy)
GER กับ apnea: ไม่ใช้ H2 blockers (ranitidine → ↑ NEC/mortality); ให้ feed thickening; จัดท่า;
Pre-discharge: apnea resolved > 5–7 วัน ก่อน discharge; home monitor ใน selected cases ถึง 43–44 wk PCA
Suspected CCHS: hypoventilation worse in sleep แต่ awake OK; test hypercapnic ventilatory response; ส่ง PHOX2B gene; consult pediatric pulmonology + neurology
10. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — Caffeine = Adenosine A1/A2a Blocker ไม่ใช่แค่ Stimulant
Caffeine ออกฤทธิ์ผ่าน competitive blockade ของ adenosine receptors → ↑ central respiratory drive + attenuate hypoxic ventilatory depression + ↓ periodic breathing. Loading 20 mg/kg → maintenance 5–10 mg/kg q24h. CAP trial: ↓ BPD (OR 0.63, NNT 10) + long-term motor benefit. เริ่มภายใน 3 วันแรก = ผลดีที่สุด

PEARL 2 — Mixed Apnea > 50% ของ Long Episodes
Long apnea ส่วนใหญ่ (> 50%) คือ mixed apnea — central pause ตาม ด้วย obstructed efforts. CPAP effective เพราะ splints pharyngeal airway. Impedance monitor ไม่ detect obstructed component → ดู bradycardia/desaturation เป็น clue. อย่าวิ่ง stimulate ทุก episode โดยไม่ check ว่า obstructed หรือเปล่า

PEARL 3 — GABA เป็น Excitatory ใน Preterm = Midazolam อาจ Paradoxical
NKCC1 > KCC2 ใน preterm neuron → intracellular Cl- สูง → GABA binding → depolarization (excitatory). Midazolam อาจ excite ไม่ใช่ sedate ใน very preterm — myoclonic jerks reported. ใช้ด้วยความระมัดระวัง; prefer morphine/fentanyl ถ้าต้องการ sedation/analgesia

PEARL 4 — Hypoxia During Apnea → Adenosine ↑ → Worsens Apnea
Hypoxia → brain adenosine ↑ (100-fold in ischemia) → A1 ↓ phrenic output + A2a → GABAergic inhibition → hypoxic ventilatory depression. Vicious cycle: AOP → hypoxia → ↑ adenosine → longer apnea. Caffeine breaks this cycle. ดังนั้น เริ่ม caffeine ก่อน apnea severe = prophylactic strategy ใน < 28 wk.

PEARL 5 — CCHS: ต้อง Test Chemical Control of Breathing
CCHS คาดว่าในทารกที่ hypoventilate ใน sleep แต่ breathe OK ขณะตื่น. ทดสอบ: hypercapnic ventilatory response (HCVR) ใน QS — ถ้า markedly impaired = CCHS จนกว่าจะ prove otherwise. ส่ง PHOX2B gene. PARM 20/25–20/27 = mild; PARM 20/28–32 = need continuous ventilation; NPARM = Haddad syndrome risk. Avoid sedatives/opioids — respiratory stimulants ไม่ได้ผล
11. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies

Schmidt B et al. N Engl J Med. 2007;357:1893 (CAP Trial): Caffeine therapy for AOP — N=2006; ↓ BPD, ↓ O2 dependency, ↓ MV duration; 18-month: ↓ CP/cognitive delay (not sustained 5 yr but ↓ DCD); early caffeine < 3 days superior.
Schmidt B et al. JAMA. 2012;307:275: CAP 5-yr follow-up — survival without disability to 5 yr; motor benefit sustained; no academic benefit.
Schmidt B et al. JAMA Pediatr. 2017;171:564: CAP 11-yr follow-up — ↓ motor impairment sustained; no academic benefit; mechanism still unclear.
Poets CF et al. JAMA. 2015;314:595: Intermittent hypoxemia/bradycardia at 36 wk → ↑ late death/disability in extremely preterm N=2500.
Di Fiore JM et al. J Pediatr. 2010;157:69: ↑ IH episodes → ↑ severe ROP in preterm cohort.
Raffay TM et al. Pediatr Res. 2019;85:318: Neonatal IH events associated with BPD diagnosis at 36 wk PMA.
Berry-Kravis EM et al. Am J Respir Crit Care Med. 2006;174:1139: CCHS — PHOX2B mutations and phenotype; PARM severity correlates with alanine repeat number.
Weese-Mayer DE et al. Am J Respir Crit Care Med. 2010;181:626 (ATS Statement): Official ATS clinical policy — CCHS genetics, diagnosis (PHOX2B sequencing), management including diaphragmatic pacing.
Kinney HC et al. Ann Rev Pathol. 2009;4:517: SIDS brainstem serotonergic abnormalities — ↓ serotonin synthesis/turnover in medulla; male sex + prematurity as risk factors.
Lodha A et al. JAMA Pediatr. 2015;169:33: Early caffeine (< 3 days) vs later → ↓ BPD, ↓ IVH, ↓ death in very preterm N > 8000.
Lemyre B et al. Cochrane 2014;CD003212: NIPPV vs NCPAP post-extubation — NIPPV reduces reintubation for persistent apnea.
Terrin G et al. Pediatrics. 2012;129:e40: Ranitidine in VLBW — associated with ↑ infections, NEC, fatal outcome; avoid H2 blockers in preterm.
Xia L et al. Respir Physiol Neurobiol. 2010;171:17: Prenatal nicotine exposure exaggerates hyperthermic enhancement of LCR in rat pups — mechanism for SIDS risk.
12. Self-Check Questions
Q: อธิบาย mechanism ที่ทำให้ GABA เป็น excitatory ใน preterm neuron และผลต่อ respiratory control

ตอบ: Preterm neuron: NKCC1 > KCC2 ratio → high intracellular Cl-. เมื่อ GABA binds GABAA receptor → Cl- channel เปิด → Cl- ออกจาก cell → membrane depolarization (excitation). With maturation: KCC2 ↑ → intracellular Cl- ↓ → GABA binding → Cl- เข้า cell → hyperpolarization (inhibition). ผลทาง clinical: midazolam ใน preterm อาจ paradoxically excite; respiratory network มี less inhibitory braking → unstable breathing, ↑ AOP.
Q: Caffeine ออกฤทธิ์ผ่าน mechanism อะไร? dose? evidence ใน neonatal outcome?

ตอบ: Mechanism: competitive blockade ของ A1 + A2a adenosine receptors → (1) ↑ central respiratory drive (↑ CO2 sensitivity); (2) ↓ hypoxic ventilatory depression; (3) ↑ diaphragmatic activity; (4) ↓ periodic breathing. Dose: caffeine citrate 20 mg/kg IV loading, 5–10 mg/kg/day q24h maintenance. Evidence: CAP Trial Schmidt NEJM 2007: ↓ BPD (OR 0.63, NNT 10), ↓ O2 dependency, ↓ MV duration. 18-month: ↓ CP/cognitive delay. 11-yr (JAMA Pediatr 2017): ↓ motor impairment sustained.
Q: ทารก CCHS จะ present อย่างไร? ยืนยันด้วยอะไร? management?

ตอบ: Present: breathes adequately awake แต่ hypoventilate ระหว่าง sleep; CO2 response impaired ใน QS; variable severity. > 90% มี PHOX2B gene mutation. Confirm: PHOX2B genetic testing; hypercapnic ventilatory response testing ใน QS. Management: PARM 20/28-32 → continuous ventilatory support (tracheostomy + MV หรือ diaphragmatic pacing); milder PARM: nocturnal ventilation only. Hirschsprung (NPARM) → 80%. Avoid sedatives/opioids; respiratory stimulants ไม่ได้ผล.
Q: Mixed apnea คืออะไรและทำไมพบบ่อยที่สุดใน long apneic episodes?

ตอบ: Mixed apnea: obstructed respiratory efforts following central pause. ช่วง > 50% ของ long apneic episodes. กลไก: central drive ↓ แรกเกิด → upper airway dilator muscles ยังไม่ activated → glottis/pharynx collapse → obstructed inspiratory efforts follow. Impedance monitoring ไม่ detect obstructed efforts (chest wall motion present) → clinical clue = bradycardia/desaturation out of proportion to pause duration. CPAP effective: splints pharynx + ↑ FRC.
Q: Laryngeal chemoreflex (LCR): ทำไม response ใน newborn อันตรายกว่า adult?

ตอบ: Adult LCR: cough + arousal + swallowing. Newborn LCR: apnea + laryngospasm + bradycardia + vasoconstriction — life-threatening. เพราะ immature post-inspiratory regulatory mechanism; baseline hypoxemia enhances severity. Afferent via superior laryngeal nerve → NTS → (1) nucleus ambiguus → thyroarytenoid contraction (laryngospasm); (2) phrenic inhibition (apnea); (3) cardiac vagal → bradycardia. Hyperthermia + prenatal nicotine prolongs LCR — both are SIDS risk factors.
Q: อธิบาย hypoxic ventilatory depression และ role ของ adenosine

ตอบ: Hypoxia initially → ↑ ventilation (peripheral chemoreceptor). After 2–3 min: ventilation drops below baseline (hypoxic roll-off) — most pronounced in fetus/newborn. Mechanism: brain adenosine ↑ during hypoxia → A1 receptor → ↓ phrenic output; A2a → activate GABAergic interneurons → ↑ inhibition. Pons also mediates depression (transection pons → sustained hyperventilation). Caffeine/aminophylline (A1+A2a blockade) attenuates hypoxic depression (Darnall Pediatr Res 1985).
Q: Consequences ของ intermittent hypoxia (IH) ใน preterm คืออะไรบ้าง?

ตอบ: IH episodes → multiple organ systems: (1) ROP: Di Fiore J Pediatr 2010 — ↑ severe ROP; (2) BPD: Raffay Pediatr Res 2019 — IH at 36 wk → BPD; (3) Neurodevelopment: Poets JAMA 2015 — IH/bradycardia → late death/disability; (4) Cardiovascular: ↑ sympathetic tone → future hypertension. Mechanism: neuronal plasticity alteration, oxidative stress, altered central chemoreceptor set-point.
Q: อธิบาย pre-Bötzinger complex (PBC) และความสัมพันธ์กับ SIDS

ตอบ: PBC: core group ของ synaptically coupled excitatory NK1R + somatostatin neurons ใน rostral VRC (medulla); pacemaker-like intrinsic bursting; essential for respiratory rhythm (progressive destruction → death in animals). Preterm infants ที่ died of SIDS: ↓ NK1R binding in NTS + PGCL (homologue of PBC) esp. male (Bright PLoS One 2017). Male sex + prematurity → less NK1R → unstable rhythmogenesis. MECP2 mutation (Rett) → ↓ NE/serotonin in medulla → dysrhythmia.
Day 42 Preview

Avery Chapter 42 — Acute Neonatal Respiratory Disorders
พรุ่งนี้เรียน Acute Neonatal Respiratory Disorders — หลังวันนี้เรียน control mechanisms แล้ว พรุ่งนี้เราจะมาดู diseases ที่เกิดจาก dysfunction ของ respiratory system ใน acute setting: RDS, TTN, MAS, PPHN, CDH, pneumonia/sepsis. โดยเฉพาะ: evaluation algorithm สำหรับ hypoxemic newborn (history → PE → O2 response → pre/postductal SpO2 → CXR → echo), surfactant physiology (Laplace's law, FRC), และ iNO indications. เชื่อมกับ Ch41: breathing pattern ที่ unstable จาก Ch41 → หาก combined กับ parenchymal/vascular disease = acute respiratory failure.
